summary: Comprehensive backend testing of Nagarik govt-scale hardening features (iteration 3). All 46 tests passed. Verified: 1) Pydantic Settings centralized config, 2) Delivery Idempotency with unique constraint on idempotency_key, 3) SSE Scoping with dual-channel broadcaster (user:{id} + role:operator).
critical_code_review_comments: Pydantic Settings properly implemented - all env vars consolidated in app/core/config.py
No os.environ or load_dotenv in app/ code (only in migrations/env.py and tests as allowed)
idempotency_key column with unique partial index (WHERE key IS NOT NULL) correctly prevents duplicate notifications
Idempotency key format '{incident_id}:{channel}:{recipient}:{escalation_level}' ensures same notification not sent twice
SSE broadcaster correctly refactored to dual-channel: user:{user_id} for guardians, role:operator for operators
Stream endpoint correctly determines channel based on user.role (operator/admin → role:operator, others → user:{id})
broadcast_incident_* methods correctly fan out to BOTH user channel AND operator channel
RBAC working correctly - guardian gets 403 on all operator endpoints
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up test notification_jobs with idempotency_key during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three govt-scale hardening features fully implemented and tested: (1) Pydantic Settings centralizes all config in app/core/config.py, (2) idempotency_key column with unique index prevents duplicate notification jobs - key format is {incident_id}:{channel}:{recipient}:{escalation_level}, (3) SSE broadcaster uses dual channels - guardians subscribe to user:{user_id}, operators to role:operator. All escalation notifications now fan out to both channels.

idempotency_column: Unique partial index on idempotency_key exists
idempotency_behavior: Duplicate idempotency_key blocked by unique constraint
Different escalation levels produce different keys (L1, L2, L3 can coexist)
sse_channel_architecture: Broadcaster has broadcast_to_user and broadcast_to_operators methods
Convenience methods fan out to BOTH user + operator channels
regression_tests: Retry/cancel endpoints return 404 for non-existent jobs
RBAC: guardian gets 403 on operator endpoints

verified_features: Pydantic Settings loads all config correctly (JWT, DB, SES, Twilio, Firebase, CORS, escalation, worker)
No os.environ or load_dotenv remains in app code (only in migrations/env.py and tests)
Backend starts cleanly with all schedulers
Notification jobs listing with idempotency_key field visible
Idempotency: enqueuing same incident+channel+recipient+level twice produces only 1 job
Idempotency: different escalation levels produce different keys
SSE stream endpoint: guardian gets user:{user_id} channel
SSE stream endpoint: operator gets role:operator channel
SSE stream endpoint: unauthenticated request returns 401
RBAC: guardian cannot access operator endpoints (403)
